name: Cold-induced PA28gamma proteasomes supported worm longevity and human-cell aggregation resistance
summary: Cold-induced PA28gamma/PSME-3 biology supplies a recent proteasome-enhancement signal, but its human relevance remains cell-based.
statement: Cold temperature induced PA28gamma/PSME-3-linked proteasome activity that was required for cold-induced C. elegans longevity and reduced disease-related protein aggregation in worm and human-cell models.
population_or_model: C. elegans temperature-shift models, HEK293 human cells, and iPSC-derived motor neuron models
time_horizon: Temperature-shift lifespan and cell-culture aggregation assays
quantitative_note: The source reports that psme-3 knockdown shortened cold-induced longevity at 15 C and that moderate cooling to 36 C increased trypsin-like activity in human cells.
caveats: The organismal longevity endpoint is in C. elegans.
Human evidence is limited to cell models and moderate-temperature pathway engagement.
Cold exposure is not equivalent to a selective proteasome-enhancing therapy.
